# Does Prior Programming Experience Affect How Students View COMP110's Pace?

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->


## Overview
For this analysis, I looked at survey data from 764 COMP110 students to explore whether students with more prior programming experience view the pacing of the course different than beginners.

## Hypothesis
I hypothesized that students with little to no prior experience would find that the course goes faster, while students with more experience would find it slower.

## Visualization

### Average Pace Rating by Level of Prior Experience
<img scr="/Final-Assignment/static/imgs/bar_graph.png" alt="Bar chart of average pace rating by level of prior experience" width="600">

### Distribution of Pace Ratings
<img src ="/Final-Assignment/static/imgs/output.png" alt="Distribution of pace ratings by level of prior experience" width="600">

### Box Plot of Pace Ratings
<img src="/Final-Assignment/static/imgs/box-plot.png" alt="Box plot of pace ratings by level of prior experience" width="600">

## Conclusion
 The data supports my theory that students with no prior experience rated the pace at around a 4.7 out of 7, while students with over 2 years of experience rated it about 3.2. I recommend COMP110 to include more practice problems at the end of the lecture slides to reinforce the topics learned.
